exemplar.title:
To what extent has the Production Linked Incentive (PLI) scheme
launched in 2020 impacted the Production growth of Indian Mobile Phone Industry?

detailedExemplarData.title:
To what extent has the Production Linked Incentive (PLI) scheme
launched in 2020 impacted the Production growth of Indian Mobile Phone Industry?

subject.title: Economics
subject.description:
**IB Economics** is a **Group 3** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **microeconomics, macroeconomics, the global economy, and development economics**, with a strong focus on **analytical and evaluative skills, real-world application of economic theory, and quantitative analysis**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2024**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| Assessment | Format | SL | HL | Weighting |
|------------|--------|----|----|-----------|
| **Paper 1** | Extended response questions | 1h 15min | 1h 30min | 30% |
| **Paper 2** | Data response questions | 1h 45min | 1h 45min | 40% (SL), 30% (HL) |
| **Paper 3** | Mathematical questions | N/A | 1h | 20% (HL only) |
| **Internal Assessment** | Portfolio of 3 commentaries | 20 hours | 20 hours | 20% |

strengths: The research question is clearly stated, tightly focused and consistently connected back to the analysis.
The introduction and signposting communicate the topic accurately and maintain clear purpose throughout the essay.
weaknesses: The methodological description lacks sufficient detail on sampling and response rates for the primary survey.
Justification for the selection of specific firms and time periods could be more fully developed to demonstrate informed selection.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Two strands (topic communication and research question) clearly meet the 5â6 band, while methodology aligns with the 3â4 band. Awarding 5 reflects the strong focus and question clarity balanced against some methodological limitations.

strengths: Economic terminology and concepts are used accurately and consistently throughout the essay.
Source material is generally relevant, with effective integration of government and industry data to support key points.
weaknesses: Critical triangulation of sources is limited, with some causal claims relying on single-source evidence.
Depth of disciplinary insight (e.g., rigorous welfare analysis) remains moderate rather than fully developed.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: While terminology use is at the highest level, application and knowledge strands sit in the 3â4 band. An overall score of 3 reflects mostly clear understanding tempered by moderate depth and reliance on descriptive citation.

strengths: Research draws consistently on appropriate, current and authoritative sources directly relevant to the research question.
Analysis employs multiple quantitative measures (CAGR calculations, charts) that underpin supported conclusions.
Discussion demonstrates a reasoned argument with partially critical evaluation, considering fiscal costs and long-term resilience.
weaknesses: Causal attribution would benefit from counterfactual or econometric analysis to isolate the PLIâs impact.
Triangulation is limited in places (e.g., global benchmark comparisons and China-plus-one effects).
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Research quality meets the top band, discussion fits the 7â9 band, and analysis sits at the upper middle band. An overall score of 7 reflects strong critical thinking with room to deepen evaluation and triangulation.

strengths: The structure follows all IB conventions with clear headings, embedded figures, and logical sequencing.
Layout and formatting (title page, table of contents, page numbering, figure captions) are consistently applied and professional.
weaknesses: Some Works Cited entries show inconsistent punctuation and formatting.
A few minor repetition and unfinished sentences do not detract from overall clarity but could be refined.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: All structural and formatting requirements are met at the highest level, warranting the full 4 points for Presentation.

strandSelections.reasoning: The research question â âTo what extent has the Production Linked Incentive (PLI) scheme launched in 2020 impacted the production growth of the Indian mobile-phone industry?â â is concise, unambiguous and tightly delimited by sector, policy and time frame.  Throughout the essay the RQ is repeatedly referenced, and all analytical sections (theoretical case, data analysis, primary survey, conclusion) connect directly back to assessing âextent of impactâ.  This satisfies the âclearly stated, focused and effectively connectedâ requirement of the 5-6 band.

The student states the focus on the Production Linked Incentive (PLI) scheme in the Indian mobile-phone sector from the very first page, defines the purpose (to evaluate its impact on production growth) and signals the relevance of the topic to Indiaâs wider macro-objectives.  The introduction, sub-heading scheme and repeated signposting (âThis research paper aims to â¦â) keep the reader oriented.  Overall, the topic is communicated accurately and with an explicitly appropriate focus, fully matching the highest rubric descriptor.

Government documents, industry reports and academic texts are integrated into the narrative and support the argument, e.g. MeitY statistics for production trends and Tragakes for subsidy theory.  Nonetheless, several citations are descriptive, and some key claims (e.g., causal attribution of growth solely to PLI) would benefit from more critical triangulation.  The overall application is mostly relevant and appropriate, fulfilling the 3-4 band criteria.

The essay follows IB EE conventions (title page, abstract, table of contents, introductionâbodyâconclusion, works cited, appendix).  Headings are logical and guide the reader effectively; diagrams and tables are embedded near relevant text.  Minor repetition of phrasing and an unfinished sentence do not compromise overall structural clarity, so the work meets the âclearly appropriateâ standard (3-4 band) and earns the full 4 points in that band.

All mandatory formatting elementsâtitle, word count, page numbering, MLA-style citations, consistent table/figure captionsâare present.  While boldface is used somewhat liberally, the overall presentation is clean, legible and professional.  The work therefore satisfies âlayout considerations present and correctly appliedâ, justifying the top 3-4 band with a score of 4.

The essay presents a structured argument culminating in a section that weighs fiscal costs, regional disparities and dependence on multinationals, demonstrating a partially critical lens.  Evaluation is integrated but occasionally descriptive and would benefit from deeper counter-argumentation or alternative explanations.  This aligns with âreasoned argument with partially critical evaluationâ, positioning the work at the lower end of the 7-9 band; a score of 7 reflects solid though not exhaustive critical engagement.

The student employs multiple data series, CAGR calculations and a subsidy diagram to analyse production and export growth, and contrasts pre- and post-PLI averages.  Conclusions generally follow from evidence, yet causality is sometimes asserted rather than demonstrated econometrically, and primary survey analysis remains surface-level.  The work therefore shows âclear analysis with mostly supported conclusionsâ, matching the 4-6 band; the upper value of 6 is merited by the breadth of quantitative treatment.

The essay demonstrates clear knowledge of Indian industrial policy, subsidy theory and the structure of the PLI.  Concepts such as infant-industry protection and strategic trade are explained accurately and referenced.  However, the depth of disciplinary insight into microeconomic welfare analysis and opportunityâcost debates remains moderate rather than extensive, so the work best fits the âknowledge is clear with partially effective use of sourcesâ descriptor (band 3-4).

The investigation draws on a wide array of current, authoritative sources (government budgets, industry associations, corporate reports, academic texts) that are directly pertinent to the RQ.  Data are up-to-date and clearly linked to each analytical section, showing âconsistently appropriate and relevantâ research selection.  This corresponds to the top 10-12 band; a score of 10 reflects small gaps in source triangulation but overall strong research quality.

The student explains the use of secondary sources (MeitY data, ICEA reports, budget documents) and a brief primary questionnaire to firms.  Source selection is generally relevant, but the justification of why particular companies or years were chosen is limited and the primary survey lacks detail on sampling or response rate.  Methodological limitations are acknowledged only briefly in the conclusion.  These features align with the âgenerally relevant â¦ some evidence of informed selectionâ description, placing the work solidly in the 3-4 band.

Economic terminology (e.g., subsidies, producer surplus, infant-industry protection, CAGR) and theoretical constructs are used accurately and consistently.  Diagrams and quantitative measures are labelled correctly, and definitions are precise.  The essay meets the highest rubric standard for consistent and accurate use of discipline-specific language.

annotations.comment: The table of contents is well structured and formatted, guiding the reader through sections and page numbering, which enhances overall navigability and professional Presentation.
The introduction effectively sets the scene by linking the PLI scheme to Indiaâs macro-objectives, demonstrating clear focus and appropriate purpose at the outset.
The statement of the research question is truncated, leaving the reader without the full articulation of scope; ensure the complete question appears to maintain clarity and focus.
The methodology section clearly outlines the research objectives and key components, showing a coherent structure linking secondary data analysis to primary research.
Primary research is mentioned but lacks detail on sampling methods and response rates; specify how firms were selected and the actual number of responses to strengthen methodological rigor.
Inclusion of a primary survey demonstrates initiative in gathering first-hand data, expanding the methodological scope beyond secondary sources.
While incentive rates are listed, there is no comparative analysis with global benchmarks; including such context would strengthen evaluation of scheme competitiveness.
Discussion of the 2021 applications and outlay uses current data to illustrate real-world impact on manufacturers, reflecting strong relevance of research.
The narrative on the PMP strategy cites a single source; triangulating this with additional industry or academic studies would enhance depth and credibility.
Figure 4 shows production growth vividly, but lacks discussion of other factors (e.g., global demand shifts) that may have influenced the trend, limiting evaluative depth.
The statement that India is the secondâlargest handset manufacturer is well supported by citation to parliamentary records, demonstrating effective use of authoritative sources.
The exports chart is compelling, but inclusion of a CAGR calculation or percentage growth annotation would clarify the rate of change over time.
Attributing production value growth solely to PLI overlooks potential impacts of global supply chain shifts; a counterfactual analysis would strengthen the evaluation.
The combined influence of PLI and China-plus-one strategies is asserted without disentangling their relative effects; consider regression or comparative analysis to isolate impacts.
The summary of company segmentation under the PLI scheme shows comprehensive research breadth across domestic and multinational beneficiaries.
The historical overview integrates industry reports and government documents to contextualize Indiaâs economic phases, showing relevant application of source material.
The theoretical explanation of subsidies is precise, with accurate use of economic terminology and clear reference to Tragakes, reflecting sound Knowledge and Understanding of concepts.
The theoretical model would benefit from explicit notation of consumer and producer surplus changes (e.g., ÎCS, ÎPS) to quantify the welfare impacts more rigorously.
Figure 1 is clear, but the discussion omits any reference to deadweight loss; include interpretation of DWL areas to deepen critical evaluation of subsidy effects.
The classification of industrial policies into general versus selective frameworks shows thorough grasp of policy concepts and application to the PLI scheme.
The stacked bar chart separating smart and feature phones provides clear insight into shifts in production composition, enhancing the analytical narrative.
Section headings are logically sequenced, guiding the reader through scheme structure, which enhances clarity of argument and Presentation.
Key details on scheme expansion and budgetary figures are integrated effectively, demonstrating mostly relevant and appropriate application of source material.
Reporting a 45% CAGR over four years demonstrates clear analysis that is directly linked to evidence, supporting the conclusions effectively.
The conclusion succinctly restates quantitative findings (132% production value increase), reinforcing well-supported and effective analysis of the PLIâs impact.
The discussion of ecosystem resilience extends evaluation beyond immediate outcomes, reflecting depth in critical thinking and consideration of long-term sustainability.
The title of the investigation is clearly stated at the start of the report, providing immediate context and aligning with the expected Formatting conventions.
A precise word count is provided on the title page (3927 words), ensuring adherence to the IB limit and satisfying the Formatting requirement.
Figure 7 effectively visualizes mobile phone production value over time with trend annotation, supporting clear analysis of policy impact.
While listing approved companies, there is no comparative performance analysis between domestic and multinational firms; adding such insights would enhance critical evaluation.
The survey results summary lacks information on sample size, response rate, and selection criteria; include these to bolster methodological transparency.
Engaging corporate IR contacts for a questionnaire demonstrates authentic initiative and personal engagement, contributing valuable primary insights.
Identifying the risk of inadequate global competitiveness shows evaluative thinking by acknowledging conditions necessary for sustained policy success.
The limitations section systematically addresses fiscal cost, regional imbalances, and MNC dependence, demonstrating a well-structured argument with critical evaluation.
The Works Cited entries display inconsistent formatting and punctuation, undermining professional presentation; standardize citation style throughout.
